10.reason: [The function call starts filling the permutation from position N down to 1, but the output prints from index 1 to N. This causes permutations to be generated in reverse order and not in lexicographical order as required.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The function call starts filling the permutation from position N down to 1, but the output prints from index 1 to N. This causes permutations to be generated in reverse order and not in lexicographical order as required.]

33.reason: [Looping from N down to 1 causes larger numbers to be tried first, which results in permutations being generated in reverse lexicographical order instead of the required lexicographical order.]

39.reason: [Assigning to a[n] where n starts at N and decreases means the last position of the array is filled first. Combined with the reverse loop, this breaks lexicographical ordering.]
